goal: Keep publish status aligned with actor log push result

summary.title: Fix publish status on push failure
summary.what: Changed publish so it validates the target draft, only marks sealed intents proposed after the actor log push succeeds, and reports actor log push failures as recoverable publish errors.
summary.why: A failed actor log push should leave the intent sealed_local because proposed means the actor log is available to other developers after sync.
summary.user_goal: Fix the earlier publish false-success issue using the standard mainline workflow.

decisions.chose: Only transition sealed_local to proposed when actor log push succeeds
decisions.rationale: This keeps local state aligned with the documented meaning of proposed.

decisions.chose: Return a recoverable PUBLISH_FAILED error when a configured origin rejects the push
decisions.rationale: Callers need the failure reason and retry guidance instead of a successful result with pushed=false.

decisions.chose: Keep local-only publish results as pushed=false without promoting state
decisions.rationale: Without a remote there is no team-visible publication.

rejected.alternative: Always mark sealed_local drafts proposed after publish is invoked
rejected.reason: That creates false proposed state when actor log push did not happen.

followups: Consider adding status fields to PublishResult so callers can display the resulting draft state.

architectural_claims: Proposed status means the sealed actor log event has been pushed to the shared remote.
behavioral_changes: mainline publish leaves sealed_local unchanged when there is no remote push.
mainline publish returns PUBLISH_FAILED when origin push fails.
mainline publish promotes sealed_local to proposed only after a successful actor log push.
